text: A total occlusion of the descending artery is observed and a coronary stent placement is not possible by this method.
A small anterior thoracotomy is performed in the left fifth intercostal space.
A special rib retractor is used to elevate the fair rib for a better visualization for the left mammary artery harvesting.
Just this graph is required so it's important that this artery is harvested without damage.
It's mandatory to mobilize the artery as high as possible to ensure an adequate length to reach the coronary artery without tension.
The camera can be useful to reach 5 to 8 more centimeters of the artery.
The anterior descending artery is identified.
The stabilization device is positioned to expose the descending artery.
A longitudinal incision is made in the coronary artery and bleeding is controlled with carbon dioxide.
The shunt is inserted and this prevents from bleeding and ischemic problems during anastomosis.
The mammary artery is prepared for bypass.
Continuous stitches of 7.0 polypropylene are used to construct an end-to-side anastomosis from the left internal mammary artery to the descending artery.
after the shunt is removed the anastomosis is finished the bulldog clamp is removed from the internal mammary artery thank you for watching this video
